title: Full-project structural index scanner and upserter
purpose: Recursively scans a project directory for supported source files and upserts their structural data into a pseudo-database for schema v2 migration or forced re-indexing.
module_context: Defines a set of excluded directories and file patterns, and sets up the main execution flow including file walking, scanning, database upserts, and git integration.

steps.content: Read the directory entries with file type information.
IF reading fails, return early without processing.
For each entry, skip if it matches any excluded name or starts with a dot.
IF the entry is a directory, recursively walk its contents.
IF the entry is a file, check if its extension is supported.
IF the file has a supported extension, add its full path to the output array.
Resolve the project root path from command line argument or current working directory.
Initialize an empty array to collect file paths.
Walk the source tree starting from the project root and populate the file list.
Log the total number of files to be scanned.
Initialize the pseudo-database with the project root.
Process each file in the list by scanning and upserting its data.
IF a scan fails, increment the error counter and continue.
IF a scan succeeds, upsert the structural data into the database and update counters.
After processing all files, attempt to checkpoint the database WAL.
Attempt to add the database file to git staging.
Log the final summary of indexed files, methods, and errors.
Exit the process with success code.
